heading: Fraud and abuse control program
label: 1320a–7c Fraud and abuse control program

children.label: A
children.text: to coordinate Federal, State, and local law enforcement programs to control fraud and abuse with respect to health plans,

children.label: B
children.text: to conduct investigations, audits, evaluations, and inspections relating to the delivery of and payment for health care in the United States,

children.label: C
children.text: to facilitate the enforcement of the provisions of sections 1320a–7, 1320a–7a, and 1320a–7b of this title and other statutes applicable to health care fraud and abuse, and

children.label: D
children.text: to provide for the modification and establishment of safe harbors and to issue advisory opinions and special fraud alerts pursuant to .section 1320a–7d of this title

children.heading: Coordination with health plans
children.label: 2 Coordination with health plans
children.text: In carrying out the program established under paragraph (1), the Secretary and the Attorney General shall consult with, and arrange for the sharing of data with representatives of health plans.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary and the Attorney General shall issue guidelines to carry out the program under paragraph (1). The provisions of sections 553, 556, and 557 of title 5 shall not apply in the issuance of such guidelines.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Such guidelines shall include guidelines relating to the furnishing of information by health plans, providers, and others to enable the Secretary and the Attorney General to carry out the program (including coordination with health plans under paragraph (2)).

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: ii Confidentiality
children.text: Such guidelines shall include procedures to assure that such information is provided and utilized in a manner that appropriately protects the confidentiality of the information and the privacy of individuals receiving health care services and items.

children.heading: Qualified immunity for providing information
children.label: iii Qualified immunity for providing information
children.text: The provisions of (relating to limitation on liability) shall apply to a person providing information to the Secretary or the Attorney General in conjunction with their performance of duties under this section.section 1320c–6(a) of this title

children.heading: Ensuring access to documentation
children.label: 4 Ensuring access to documentation
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services is authorized to exercise such authority described in paragraphs (3) through (9) of as necessary with respect to the activities under the fraud and abuse control program established under this subsection.section 406(a) of title 5

children.heading: Authority of Inspector General
children.label: 5 Authority of Inspector General
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to diminish the authority of any Inspector General, including such authority as provided in chapter 4 of title 5.

children.heading: Public-private partnership for waste, fraud, and abuse detection
children.label: 6 Public-private partnership for waste, fraud, and abuse detection

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under the program described in paragraph (1), there is established a public-private partnership (in this paragraph referred to as the “partnership”) of health plans, Federal and State agencies, law enforcement agencies, health care anti-fraud organizations, and any other entity determined appropriate by the Secretary (in this paragraph referred to as “partners”) for purposes of detecting and preventing health care waste, fraud, and abuse.

children.heading: Contract with trusted third party
children.label: B Contract with trusted third party
children.text: In carrying out the partnership, the Secretary shall enter into a contract with a trusted third party for purposes of carrying out the duties of the partnership described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: i
children.text: provide technical and operational support to facilitate data sharing between partners in the partnership;

children.label: ii
children.text: analyze data so shared to identify fraudulent and aberrant billing patterns;

children.label: iii
children.text: conduct aggregate analyses of health care data so shared across Federal, State, and private health plans for purposes of detecting fraud, waste, and abuse schemes;

children.label: iv
children.text: identify outlier trends and potential vulnerabilities of partners in the partnership with respect to such schemes;

children.label: v
children.text: refer specific cases of potential unlawful conduct to appropriate governmental entities;

children.label: vi
children.text: convene, not less than annually, meetings with partners in the partnership for purposes of providing updates on the partnership’s work and facilitating information sharing between the partners;

children.label: vii
children.text: enter into data sharing and data use agreements with partners in the partnership in such a manner so as to ensure the partnership has access to data necessary to identify waste, fraud, and abuse while maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of such data;

children.label: viii
children.text: provide partners in the partnership with plan-specific, confidential feedback on any aberrant billing patterns or potential fraud identified by the partnership with respect to such partner;

children.label: ix
children.text: establish a process by which entities described in subparagraph (A) may enter the partnership and requirements such entities must meet to enter the partnership;

children.label: x
children.text: provide appropriate training, outreach, and education to partners based on the results of data analyses described in clauses (ii) and (iii); and

children.label: xi
children.text: perform such other duties as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Substance use disorder treatment analysis
children.label: D Substance use disorder treatment analysis
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the trusted third party with a contract in effect under subparagraph (B) shall perform an analysis of aberrant or fraudulent billing patterns and trends with respect to providers and suppliers of substance use disorder treatments from data shared with the partnership.December 27, 2020

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: There shall be an executive board of the partnership comprised of representatives of the Federal Government and representatives of the private sector selected by the Secretary.

children.heading: Chairs
children.label: II Chairs
children.text: The executive board shall be co-chaired by one Federal Government official and one representative from the private sector.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: ii Meetings
children.text: The executive board of the partnership shall meet at least once per year.

children.label: I
children.text: Providing strategic direction for the partnership, including membership criteria and a mission statement.

children.label: II
children.text: Communicating with the leadership of the Department of Health and Human Services and the Department of Justice and the various private health sector associations.

children.label: i
children.text: a review of activities conducted by the partnership over the 2-year period ending on the date of the submission of such report, including any progress to any objectives established by the partnership;

children.label: ii
children.text: any savings voluntarily reported by health plans participating in the partnership attributable to the partnership during such period;

children.label: iii
children.text: any savings to the Federal Government attributable to the partnership during such period;

children.label: iv
children.text: any other outcomes attributable to the partnership, as determined by the Secretary, during such period; and

children.label: v
children.text: a strategic plan for the 2-year period beginning on the day after the date of the submission of such report, including a description of any emerging fraud and abuse schemes, trends, or practices that the partnership intends to study during such period.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: G Funding
children.text: The partnership shall be funded by amounts otherwise made available to the Secretary for carrying out the program described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Transitional provisions
children.label: H Transitional provisions
children.text: To the extent consistent with this subsection, all functions, personnel, assets, liabilities, and administrative actions applicable on the date before , to the National Fraud Prevention Partnership established on , by charter of the Secretary shall be transferred to the partnership established under subparagraph (A) as of .December 27, 2020September 17, 2012December 27, 2020

children.heading: Nonapplicability of FACA
children.label: I Nonapplicability of FACA
children.text: The provisions of the Federal Advisory Committee Act shall not apply to the partnership established by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: J Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement the partnership established by subparagraph (A) by program instruction or otherwise.

children.label: i
children.text: demonstrates the capability to carry out the duties of the partnership described in subparagraph (C);

children.label: ii
children.text: complies with such conflict of interest standards determined appropriate by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: meets such other requirements as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Additional use of funds by Inspector General
children.label: b Additional use of funds by Inspector General

children.heading: Reimbursements for investigations
children.label: 1 Reimbursements for investigations
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services is authorized to receive and retain for current use reimbursement for the costs of conducting investigations and audits and for monitoring compliance plans when such costs are ordered by a court, voluntarily agreed to by the payor, or otherwise.

children.heading: Crediting
children.label: 2 Crediting
children.text: Funds received by the Inspector General under paragraph (1) as reimbursement for costs of conducting investigations shall be deposited to the credit of the appropriation from which initially paid, or to appropriations for similar purposes currently available at the time of deposit, and shall remain available for obligation for 1 year from the date of the deposit of such funds.
a policy of health insurance;
a contract of a service benefit organization; and
a membership agreement with a health maintenance organization or other prepaid health plan.
